description: Thriller requires element configurations that sustain tension through information asymmetry, capable antagonists, and escalating stakes with a ticking clock.

character_constraints.description: A resourceful character under pressure who must outthink or outmaneuver the threat.
A formidable opposing force whose plan creates the central threat — must be competent and dangerous.
A partner or informant who provides critical information or support under pressure.
A character whose true allegiance is unclear — creates paranoia and suspense.

relationship_constraints.description: Direct opposition between protagonist and antagonist where the stakes are existential.
A trusted figure who turns out to be working against the protagonist.

place_constraints.description: The antagonist's base of operations or the location of the climactic confrontation.
A world of danger and deception where normal rules don't apply.

object_constraints.description: The thing everyone is pursuing — plans, codes, evidence, a person — that drives the chase.
Evidence or intelligence that shifts the power balance when obtained.
Tools of the trade — may be literal weapons or technological/informational assets.

element_rules.description: The antagonist must be established as a credible, active threat — not passive or incompetent.
element_rules.testable_condition: The antagonist appears as participant in at least 3 timeline moments and initiates at least 1 transition.

element_rules.description: Information asymmetry must exist — the protagonist and antagonist must each know things the other doesn't.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least 2 'learns' or 'reveals' transitions appear in the timeline, changing the power dynamic.

element_rules.description: Stakes must escalate — the consequences of failure must increase across the story.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'loses' transition occurs in the second half of the timeline that raises the stakes beyond the initial threat.
